name: ICT-Enabled Citizen Accountability and Grievance Redressal
description: Interventions using voice forums, helplines, digital records, and new media to increase transparency and hold local governments/bureaucracies accountable for service delivery. Spans ethnographic studies of citizen journalism platforms, bureaucratic subversion of accountability tools, and social audit movements. Recurring in Indian and other Global South governance contexts.

name: Datafication of Informal Urban Settlements
description: Research and interventions that render slums and informal settlements 'visible' through data collection, mapping, and datafication, often framed through data justice and community-based approaches. Spans the ethics/power dynamics of who collects data about marginalized urban populations and how it reshapes citizenship. Includes smart-city citizenship debates and applied data justice frameworks.

name: Satellite Imagery for Development Measurement and Targeting
description: Use of remote sensing, nighttime luminosity, and land-cover classification to measure economic activity, detect slums, monitor urban growth, and target rural development. Combines CS methods (image classification datasets, models) with impact evaluation and vulnerability mapping. Recurs across poverty measurement, urban planning, and flood/disaster risk applications.

name: Political Connections, Patronage, and Rent Capture
description: Empirical work documenting how political connections shape firm access to credit, contracts, and resources, and how election finance links builders/industry to politicians. Spans problem-discovery accounts of elite power and quantitative impact evaluations of favoritism. Focused on capture of state resources by connected actors.

name: Electoral Cycles in Public Service Provision
description: Quasi-experimental studies of how election timing distorts the provision of public goods such as electricity, measuring political manipulation of infrastructure and service losses. Combines CS/data methods with causal identification strategies. A tight cluster around electoral incentives and service delivery in India.

name: Elite Power and Structural Inequality (Foundational Critique)
description: Canonical texts theorizing concentration of economic and political power, class domination, and rising inequality that frame governance failures. Primarily problem-discovery framing readings rather than interventions. Provides the diagnostic lens for why governance is captured or fails marginalized groups.

name: E-Participation and Digital Democracy Platforms
description: Studies of government-provided platforms and apps designed to solicit citizen engagement, participation, and trust in democratic/civic processes. Spans smart-city engagement apps, e-participation platforms, and factors affecting citizen uptake. Recurs across African and Global South municipal contexts with attention to mistrust and adoption.

name: Digital Identity, Documentation, and Exclusion
description: Interventions and critiques around large-scale digital identification and civil registration systems (e.g., Aadhaar, birth declaration) and how they produce inclusion or exclusion for marginalized populations. Spans documentary evidence of denial-of-service and design frameworks for last-mile documentation. Strong marginalization/caste/gender axes.

name: ICT for Refugee and Stateless Populations
description: Ethnographic and design research on how displaced and stateless communities use ICTs for solidarity, leadership, and democratic aspirations under constrained conditions. Focused on the Rohingya and similar refugee contexts. Combines sociotechnical dynamics with questions of governance, voice, and belonging.

name: Media, Information, and Policy Discourse
description: Computational and empirical analysis of media bias, mass media influence on welfare scheme performance, and political polarization/blogosphere structure. Spans CS network/text analysis of political communication and impact evaluations of media as accountability mechanism. Recurs across US polarization studies and Indian policy discourse.

name: Open Data and Aid Transparency
description: Interventions and visions around open data for international aid, SDG tracking, and development transparency. Spans classification/tracking of aid contributions with CS methods and advocacy framing of open data changing accountability. Focused on making resource flows visible and measurable.

name: Community Telecenters and Intermediaries
description: Studies of rural telecenters and community multimedia centers as governance/access infrastructure, focusing on sustainability failures and the complex role of human intermediaries. Combines operations/scale challenges with sociotechnical analysis of who mediates access. A recurring ICT4D deployment pattern.
